tagline: An FCC-ready analyst workspace for provider footprints, competitive overlap, speed tiers, and demographic benchmarking across the top U.S. fixed-broadband players.
heroNarrative: This first build sets up the provider universe, module structure, download center, and FCC/Census data model so the site can move quickly from watchlist mode into live availability analysis once your FCC account and source files are ready.

statusCards.label: Build Stage
statusCards.detail: UI, exports, and metric taxonomy are in place.

statusCards.label: FCC Access
statusCards.detail: Public downloads can load first; API/Fabric enrichments can follow.

statusCards.label: Granularity Path
statusCards.detail: The schema is staged for state, county, tract, and location-level joins.

statusCards.label: Exports
statusCards.detail: Current watchlist and metadata can already be downloaded to Excel-compatible files.

dataSources.name: FCC BDC availability downloads
dataSources.detail: Primary source for provider, technology, speed, and residential/business availability records.

dataSources.name: FCC Broadband Map API / authenticated public data access
dataSources.detail: Account-backed access path for more programmatic pulls once your FCC credentials are set up.

dataSources.name: FCC Fabric / CostQuest location enrichment
dataSources.detail: Needed for the most granular serviceable-location workflows and exact address joins.

dataSources.name: Census / ACS / TIGER enrichments
dataSources.detail: Required to reproduce the demographic, housing, business, density, and morphology views in your screenshots.

analysisViews.name: Provider Footprint
analysisViews.description: Core footprint tables by geography, technology, and speed tier.

analysisViews.name: Market Size
analysisViews.description: Market-sizing tables for population, housing units, businesses, and passings.

analysisViews.name: Demographics
analysisViews.description: Population growth, housing, density, and morphology benchmarks layered onto the footprint.

analysisViews.name: Competitive Intensity
analysisViews.description: Counts of locations with zero, one, two, three, four, or five-plus competitors.

analysisViews.name: Overlap by Technology
analysisViews.description: Wired-only and wired-plus-FWA overlap by copper, cable, fiber, FWA, and none.

analysisViews.name: Overlap by Provider
analysisViews.description: Top competitor tables split by all-tech, ILEC, MSO, fiber overbuilder, and FWA cohorts.

analysisViews.name: Speed Mix
analysisViews.description: Availability bands ranging from sub-25/3 to gig-plus categories.

analysisViews.name: Download Center
analysisViews.description: Direct export of watchlists, metric dictionaries, generated snapshots, and Excel workbooks.

metricFamilies.name: Technology overlap
metricFamilies.description: Fiber only, cable only, cable plus fiber, copper only, copper plus fiber, copper plus cable, copper plus cable plus fiber, FWA, FWA overlap, and locations with no qualifying competitor.

metricFamilies.name: Competitive scenario
metricFamilies.description: Zero competitors, one competitor, two competitors, three competitors, four competitors, and five competitors or more.

metricFamilies.name: Demographics
metricFamilies.description: Population, growth rate, housing units, businesses, state area, road miles, household density, and morphology.

roadmap.title: Load the first FCC public availability files
roadmap.detail: Once your account is created, we can import the first provider/state download set and replace placeholder readiness cards with live tables.

roadmap.title: Normalize provider naming and categories
roadmap.detail: We will map FCC legal-entity names into analyst-friendly parent brands and competitor cohorts.

roadmap.title: Join Census enrichments
roadmap.detail: This will unlock the market-size and demographic sheets that mirror your workbook examples.

roadmap.title: Add licensed Fabric support if available
roadmap.detail: Only needed if you want repeatable exact-address or serviceable-location workflows at full granularity.

providers.name: AT&T
providers.notes: Core incumbent footprint with both copper legacy and fiber build relevance.

providers.name: Verizon
providers.notes: Important for FiOS footprint and fixed-wireless overlap analysis.

providers.name: Comcast Xfinity
providers.notes: Largest cable benchmark for overlap and speed-tier comparisons.

providers.name: Charter Spectrum
providers.notes: Critical MSO competitor across a wide state and county footprint.

providers.name: Cox
providers.notes: Meaningful in Southeast and Southwest market studies.

providers.name: Altice USA / Optimum
providers.notes: Needed for Northeast and select Southern overlap work.

providers.name: Cable One / Sparklight
providers.notes: Matches the benchmark-style workbook views in your screenshots.

providers.name: Breezeline
providers.notes: Relevant for East Coast and former WOW market comparisons.

providers.name: Astound Broadband
providers.notes: Important consolidated brand across several metro systems.

providers.name: Brightspeed
providers.notes: Key post-Lumen incumbent footprint to track separately.

providers.name: Frontier
providers.notes: Major rural and suburban telco competitor with active fiber builds.

providers.name: Lumen / Quantum Fiber
providers.notes: Needs parent-brand mapping because FCC entities may not align with retail branding.

providers.name: Windstream / Kinetic
providers.notes: Useful in rural competitive-intensity and copper transition analysis.

providers.name: Consolidated Communications
providers.notes: Include alongside Fidium branding once naming normalization is in place.

providers.name: altafiber / Cincinnati Bell
providers.notes: Explicitly included per your request.

providers.name: Metronet
providers.notes: Important overbuilder for fair-share and penetration studies.

providers.name: Mediacom
providers.notes: Needed for Midwest and smaller-market cable benchmarking.

providers.name: TDS Telecom
providers.notes: Useful in state-by-state rural footprint work.

providers.name: Midco
providers.notes: Meaningful for Dakotas and upper Midwest competitive context.

providers.name: WideOpenWest (WOW!)
providers.notes: Smaller but still relevant in selected overlap markets.

providers.name: Ziply Fiber
providers.notes: Pacific Northwest focus with active fiber upgrade story.

providers.name: Hawaiian Telcom
providers.notes: Important for island-specific market and grant analysis.

providers.name: Google Fiber
providers.notes: High-visibility urban benchmark even with selective footprint.

providers.name: Armstrong
providers.notes: Useful for smaller-market cable and hybrid footprint comparisons.

providers.name: GVTC
providers.notes: A good example of a dense regional operator with overbuild relevance.

providers.name: GoNetspeed
providers.notes: Represents newer fiber overbuilder expansion patterns.

providers.name: Point Broadband
providers.notes: Useful in Southeast and smaller-market fiber studies.

providers.name: Ritter Communications
providers.notes: Good fit for enterprise-heavy and rural Southern footprints.

providers.name: T-Mobile Home Internet
providers.notes: Included because FWA overlap shows up throughout your workbook examples.

providers.name: Starlink
providers.notes: Useful as a fallback competitor layer, especially in sparse geographies.
